visa_types.name: Long Term D Type Visa
visa_types.overview:
Long Term D Type Visa

Please note:
Applicants seeking to apply LONG STAY VISA – VISA TYPE D and Family Members of EU Nationals seeking to apply C-Type Visa do not require to book a prior online appointment and can attend the centre as Walk-In.
Effective March 15, 2025, applicants will now be required to submit their visa requests no earlier than three months before and no later than three months after the start date of their temporary residence or residence and work permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Long Term D Type Visa

Please note:
Applicants seeking to apply LONG STAY VISA – VISA TYPE D and Family Members of EU Nationals seeking to apply C-Type Visa do not require to book a prior online appointment and can attend the centre as Walk-In.
Effective March 15, 2025, applicants will now be required to submit their visa requests no earlier than three months before and no later than three months after the start date of their temporary residence or residence and work permit.

visa_types.name: Short Term C Type Visa
visa_types.overview:
Please note:
Effective 6th March 2023, applicants seeking to apply SHORT-TERM STAY - VISA TYPE C must book a prior online appointment.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission.
visa_types.full_text:
Please note:
Effective 6th March 2023, applicants seeking to apply SHORT-TERM STAY - VISA TYPE C must book a prior online appointment.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission.

visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and confirm to the same while submitting the application. The applicant may, however, be asked to provide additional documents, if required, by the Embassy. 
Please see the attached Business Visa checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Q1Ir0x0z86OcOIXvHNjsn/b01a9b45342c274083a77843ae468dd7/Business_Cultural_Sports-UPDATED-Oct-25.pdf)
Letter of Guarantee from a Legal Person in Croatia. Click Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/UserDocsImages/dokumenti/obrasci/211104-Jamstveno-pismo-pra.pdf?vel=314551)
The Letter of Guarantee must be presented in copy when applying for visa. The original must be duly attested either by a Notary Public in Croatia or by the competent Croatian Consulate. It must also be deposited either with the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs (MVEP) or directly with the Consulate, accompanied by the supporting documentation.
Detailed information on the procedure is available at the following link https://mvep.gov.hr/informacije-za-gradjane-244593/konzularne-informacije-22730/vize-22733/postupak-izdavanja-viza/jamstveno-pismo/22755 (https://mvep.gov.hr/informacije-za-gradjane-244593/konzularne-informacije-22730/vize-22733/postupak-izdavanja-viza/jamstveno-pismo/22755)
Kindly click here to download the Schengen Visa Applicant Questionnaire (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1vjR0PiQayVIgBXcFP6pOv/d5fcacd6814280bc2cee7a66e655bc35/questionnaire.pdf) (use Fill & Sign option in Adobe Reader to add details)
visa_types.visa_fees:
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Business]
Please be informed that the fees charged by the Consulate General or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member
                                                                            States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- For further details regarding visa fee exemption please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas - Visa Requirements Overview: click here (http://www.mvep.hr/en/consular-information/visas/visa-requirements-overview/).
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time for the visa application is 15 working days and can be extended to 45 working days at the Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), UAE National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai, UAE may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time. 
Applications will be accepted maximum 180 days prior to the travel date. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.forms:
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian) 
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- UAE Visa details
- Host/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Please find attached the Schengen Visa Questionnaire. This is a requirement by Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai. (Use Fill and Sign option on Adobe Reader to fill this form along with other supporting documents)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
Holders of UAE Ordinary/ Diplomatic / Official Passports do not require a visa to Croatia. Read Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879?country=143)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
Holders of UAE Ordinary/ Diplomatic / Official Passports do not require a visa to Croatia. Read Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879?country=143)

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Business]
Please be informed that the fees charged by the Consulate General or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member
                                                                            States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- For further details regarding visa fee exemption please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas - Visa Requirements Overview: click here (http://www.mvep.hr/en/consular-information/visas/visa-requirements-overview/).

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and confirm to the same while submitting the application. The applicant may, however, be asked to provide additional documents, if required, by the Embassy. 
Please see the attached Business Visa checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Q1Ir0x0z86OcOIXvHNjsn/b01a9b45342c274083a77843ae468dd7/Business_Cultural_Sports-UPDATED-Oct-25.pdf)
Letter of Guarantee from a Legal Person in Croatia. Click Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/UserDocsImages/dokumenti/obrasci/211104-Jamstveno-pismo-pra.pdf?vel=314551)
The Letter of Guarantee must be presented in copy when applying for visa. The original must be duly attested either by a Notary Public in Croatia or by the competent Croatian Consulate. It must also be deposited either with the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs (MVEP) or directly with the Consulate, accompanied by the supporting documentation.
Detailed information on the procedure is available at the following link https://mvep.gov.hr/informacije-za-gradjane-244593/konzularne-informacije-22730/vize-22733/postupak-izdavanja-viza/jamstveno-pismo/22755 (https://mvep.gov.hr/informacije-za-gradjane-244593/konzularne-informacije-22730/vize-22733/postupak-izdavanja-viza/jamstveno-pismo/22755)
Kindly click here to download the Schengen Visa Applicant Questionnaire (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1vjR0PiQayVIgBXcFP6pOv/d5fcacd6814280bc2cee7a66e655bc35/questionnaire.pdf) (use Fill & Sign option in Adobe Reader to add details)

## Photo Specifications

Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious, ethnic or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
- children in photographs must be alone, and their faces fully visible and eyes open
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photos provided do not follow these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
The average processing time for the visa application is 15 working days and can be extended to 45 working days at the Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), UAE National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai, UAE may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time. 
Applications will be accepted maximum 180 days prior to the travel date. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian) 
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- UAE Visa details
- Host/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Please find attached the Schengen Visa Questionnaire. This is a requirement by Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai. (Use Fill and Sign option on Adobe Reader to fill this form along with other supporting documents)

visa_types.name: Work
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
The Visa Application Form must be filled online and signed by the applicant. Click Here (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en)
Please click here for Long Stay – D visa checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4xAM0tEFSnOp0ggFMVVJJI/890691424a5a33e4c4b77f72a54119ef/Long-Stay-Croatia-Updated-Oct-25.pdf)
visa_types.visa_fees:
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Work]
IMPORTANT INFORMATION
Applicable visa fee, in United Arab Emirates Dirham (AED) is as per the current exchange rate and may be subject to change without notice.
VFS service fee (inclusive of VAT) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.
All fees can be paid by cash or card.
All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time for the visa application is 15 working days at the Embassy. The Visa Application along with Travel Document is sent to the Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai for consideration and processing. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days. 
Application logistics time apart from Embassy's own processing time may vary and may affect the total processing time of the visa applications. Expected & Effective processing time is 4 to 8 weeks.
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), UAE National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time. 
Applications will be accepted maximum 90 days prior to the travel date.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.forms:
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant. 
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en) 
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- UAE Visa details
- Host/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Not Uploading documents will affect your visa application decision.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A long-stay visa (D visa) is an entry permit if the third-country national has been approved for a temporary residence permit or a residence and work permit and if a visa is required to enter the Republic of Croatia.
An application for a category “D” visa is submitted in person.
Effective March 15, 2025, applicants will now be required to submit their visa requests no earlier than three months before and no later than three months after the start date of their temporary residence or residence and work permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A long-stay visa (D visa) is an entry permit if the third-country national has been approved for a temporary residence permit or a residence and work permit and if a visa is required to enter the Republic of Croatia.
An application for a category “D” visa is submitted in person.
Effective March 15, 2025, applicants will now be required to submit their visa requests no earlier than three months before and no later than three months after the start date of their temporary residence or residence and work permit.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Work]
IMPORTANT INFORMATION
Applicable visa fee, in United Arab Emirates Dirham (AED) is as per the current exchange rate and may be subject to change without notice.
VFS service fee (inclusive of VAT) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.
All fees can be paid by cash or card.
All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
The Visa Application Form must be filled online and signed by the applicant. Click Here (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en)
Please click here for Long Stay – D visa checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4xAM0tEFSnOp0ggFMVVJJI/890691424a5a33e4c4b77f72a54119ef/Long-Stay-Croatia-Updated-Oct-25.pdf)

## Photo Specifications

Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the presented photos do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
The average processing time for the visa application is 15 working days at the Embassy. The Visa Application along with Travel Document is sent to the Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai for consideration and processing. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days. 
Application logistics time apart from Embassy's own processing time may vary and may affect the total processing time of the visa applications. Expected & Effective processing time is 4 to 8 weeks.
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), UAE National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time. 
Applications will be accepted maximum 90 days prior to the travel date.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant. 
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en) 
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- UAE Visa details
- Host/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Not Uploading documents will affect your visa application decision.

This must be stamped by legal person and certified by notary public in Croatia. Original Letter of Guarantee must be submitted for Visa Application.

                                                                            States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,

Letter of Guarantee from a Legal Person in Croatia. Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6rxp4AWk5cjdZimcuSt5XF/aa51da4e101735dcbccd89cff92a9e9b/211104-Jamstveno-pismo-pra__5_.pdf)
This must be stamped by legal person and certified by notary public in Croatia. Original Letter of Guarantee must be submitted for Visa Application.
Kindly click here to download the Schengen Visa Applicant Questionnaire (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1vjR0PiQayVIgBXcFP6pOv/d5fcacd6814280bc2cee7a66e655bc35/questionnaire.pdf) (use Fill & Sign option in Adobe Reader to add details)

## Photo Specifications

Photographs must follow the below guidelines
- in colour
- 35x45 mm
- printed on normal photographic paper
- taken against a light background
- full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to the head of hair
- the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed
- The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead
- children in photographs must be alone, and their faces fully visible and eyes open.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the presented photos do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
The average processing time for the visa application is 15 working days and can be extended to 45 working days at the Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai. However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended up to 60 days. 
Eid, Summer holidays (July and August), UAE National Holidays, Christmas, and New Year may extend the visa application processing time. VFS/Mission’s closures and holidays may extend the visa application processing time.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
IMPORTANT NOTE:
In order to avoid any delays in the visa application process, it is paramount that the visa application is complete, and the documents submitted are as per the Mission’s requirements. The Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai, UAE may request additional documents, and this may lead to further delay in visa application processing time. 
Applications will be accepted maximum 180 days prior to the travel date. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form duly filled Online only in English & signed by the applicant.
(For minors under age of 18: signed by parent(s) or legal guardian)
Online Application Form (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/default.aspx?langId=en)
- Uploading Photograph is a must.
- Uploading the supporting documents as per the document checklist is a must.
- All items on the application are mandatory;
- UAE Visa details
- Host/ Hotel Details and Contact Details
- Contact Details for the applicant.
- Please find attached the Schengen Visa Questionnaire. This is a requirement by Consulate General of Croatia in Dubai. (Use Fill and Sign option on Adobe Reader to fill this form along with other supporting documents)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
Holders of UAE Ordinary/ Diplomatic / Official Passports do not require a visa to Croatia. Read Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879?country=143)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
Holders of UAE Ordinary/ Diplomatic / Official Passports do not require a visa to Croatia. Read Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879?country=143)
Croatia Visa Application center in Dubai is now accepting Tourism application with the below conditions in addition to normal requirements in the Tourism checklist.
Applicants traveling for tourist reasons must provide a certificate or receipt of paid accommodation in a hotel, camp, private renter or rented vessel and other form of tourist accommodation is presented.
Application without full payment confirmation and receipt of accommodation will not be accepted. Please be informed that the following condition does not guarantee the approval of the visa, it remains Embassy decision on the same.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Short-term visa (visa C) - approval for transit through or intended stay in the territory of Croatia for a duration of no more than 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is issued for one, two or more entries for transit, tourist, business, private or other purposes. The validity period of a visa depends on the circumstances of traveling and cannot exceed five years.
The third-country national may not work in the territory of Croatia based on a short-term visa (visa C) alone.
Short-term visa (visa C) application shall be submitted in person. Visa application shall be submitted no more than six months.
Holders of UAE Ordinary/ Diplomatic / Official Passports do not require a visa to Croatia. Read Here (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879?country=143)
Croatia Visa Application center in Dubai is now accepting Tourism application with the below conditions in addition to normal requirements in the Tourism checklist.
Applicants traveling for tourist reasons must provide a certificate or receipt of paid accommodation in a hotel, camp, private renter or rented vessel and other form of tourist accommodation is presented.
Application without full payment confirmation and receipt of accommodation will not be accepted. Please be informed that the following condition does not guarantee the approval of the visa, it remains Embassy decision on the same.

Fees are due on the visa application when applying.
